natural_language_analysis.summary: Both return witnesses are semantically valid. Select the second disjunct, destruct suffix, unfold xizi_dll_suffix and xizi_dll_next_result, use the branch equality or contradiction supplied by PreH1 and PreH4, and return the two xizi_dllseg resources unchanged.

vc_shape.pre_spatial: xizi_dllseg first linklist_pre linklist_node_pre before prefix ** xizi_dllseg next linklist_node_pre linklist_pre last suffix in the selected second disjunct
vc_shape.pre_pure: PreH1: next = linklist_pre; PreH4: xizi_dll_suffix linklist_pre next suffix; non-null facts PreH2 and PreH3 are available but unnecessary
vc_shape.pre_exists: none needed; the segment predicates remain folded
vc_shape.post_spatial: the identical pair of xizi_dllseg resources

witnesses.witness_instantiation: No right-side existential is required. Destruct suffix. For nil, xizi_dll_next_result nil reduces to NULL, hence zero. For first0 :: rest, unfolding xizi_dll_suffix in PreH4 gives next = first0 and first0 <> linklist_pre, contradicting PreH1 next = linklist_pre.
witnesses.space_plan: Choose the second disjunct and preserve both xizi_dllseg conjuncts by identity/cancellation. No segment unfolding, splitting, merging, or new ownership is needed.
witnesses.pure_plan: Destruct suffix and unfold xizi_dll_suffix in PreH4 plus xizi_dll_next_result in the goal. The nil case closes by reduction of NULL to zero. The cons case is impossible because PreH4 yields next = first0 and first0 <> linklist_pre while PreH1 yields next = linklist_pre.
witnesses.refinement_plan: not applicable; this VC has no safeExec or abstract refinement state

premise_discharge.discharged_by: definition reduction of xizi_dll_next_result; NULL is the zero address

premise_discharge.premise: suffix = first0 :: rest is impossible under next = linklist_pre
premise_discharge.discharged_by: the cons clause of PreH4 supplies next = first0 and first0 <> linklist_pre, contradicting PreH1
identity/cancellation of both unchanged xizi_dllseg antecedents

analysis_groups.shared_proof_pattern: Select the second return-witness disjunct, destruct suffix, unfold xizi_dll_suffix and xizi_dll_next_result, close one branch by definition reduction and the other from the sentinel equality/inequality contradiction, then frame both xizi_dllseg predicates unchanged.
analysis_groups.shared_space_plan: Both xizi_dllseg resources are returned verbatim and can be discharged by identity/cancellation without unfolding.
analysis_groups.shared_pure_plan: Both proofs use only the two clauses of xizi_dll_suffix, the two clauses of xizi_dll_next_result, and PreH1; no arithmetic or extra premise is required.
analysis_groups.grouping_reason: The two witnesses are from the same function and share one compact suffix-destructor/framing proof pattern; the combined size 2 is below the controller maximum 12 and no helper boundary or dependency requires a split.

proof_groups.proof_strategy: For each witness unfold the return-witness definition, select the second disjunct, introduce parameters, destruct suffix, unfold xizi_dll_suffix in PreH4 and reduce xizi_dll_next_result. In return_wit_1 the nil case yields zero and the cons case contradicts PreH1; in return_wit_2 the nil case contradicts PreH1 and the cons case yields the required next equality. Preserve both xizi_dllseg conjuncts by identity/cancellation.

per_witness_plan.proof_of_xizi_double_link_next_return_wi: right; intros; destruct suffix; nil closes by next-result reduction and spatial identity; cons closes by contradiction between PreH1 and the cons clause of PreH4
right; intros; destruct suffix; nil contradicts PreH1 via the nil clause of PreH4; cons obtains next = head-of-suffix from PreH4; cancel the unchanged segment pair
